MRNA.title: Moderna's Flu Vaccine Clears Key FDA Panel Vote As Advisory Committee Backs Shot 9-0

macro_events.event_summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh, appointed by Trump with expectations of rate cuts, signals a more hawkish stance than anticipated — pushing back against near-term cuts.
primary_drivers: Higher-for-longer rate path raises discount rates and pressures long-duration assets
Stronger USD and higher real yields
Net interest margin support for banks; pressure on rate-sensitive borrowers

beneficiaries.rationale: Higher-for-longer rates support net interest margin on its massive deposit base; large-cap money center bank is the cleanest beneficiary of a steeper/higher rate curve without the duration risk smaller banks carry.
Schwab earns substantial spread income on client cash sweeps; a delay in cuts directly supports NIM and reduces cash-sorting headwinds that plagued the stock in 2023-24.

Hawkish Fed surprise reduces refi/issuance tailwind but signals Fed independence/credibility — Warsh's hawkish framing supports the long-duration credibility premium; secondary play, lower confidence.

losers.rationale: High-multiple growth name whose valuation depends heavily on terminal cash flows discounted at lower rates; hawkish surprise compresses the multiple of the most extended momentum names.
Realty Income — REITs trade as bond proxies; higher-for-longer rates raise their cost of capital and make their dividend yield less attractive vs Treasuries.

headlines: Trump picked Kevin Warsh to cut rates. The new Fed chief just told us he has other plans.
